skill.name: competitor-analysis-bot
skill.description: Automated competitor data collection with SWOT analysis and gap reporting for strategic decision making.
skill.summary: Automate competitor research with AI-powered SWOT analysis, feature comparison, and gap reports.
skill.author: Hermes-Sedimentary

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged two HIGH-severity patterns: weak cryptographic algorithm (line 4) and high file entropy (line 1). Both are false positives. Line 4 contains only a Chinese-language description string with no cryptography. The elevated entropy (6.23 bits) is expected for UTF-8 Chinese text, not binary or encrypted content. The skill contains no executable code, no network requests, no file system access, and no environment variable usage. It is a pure prompt template in markdown format. No security risks identified after evaluation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm (FALSE POSITIVE)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer detected 'weak cryptographic algorithm' on line 4. Line 4 contains Chinese description text '竞品自动分析 — 输入竞品名单→自动采集数据+SWOT分析+差距报告' which is a plain text description of the skill functionality. No cryptographic code exists anywhere in this file. Confirmed false positive.

dangerous_patterns.title: High file entropy (FALSE POSITIVE)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged high entropy (6.23 bits) as possible binary or encrypted content. This entropy level is normal for UTF-8 encoded Chinese text, where multi-byte characters naturally produce higher entropy than ASCII. The file is plain readable markdown. Confirmed false positive.

content.user_title: Automate Competitor Research and SWOT Analysis
content.value_statement: Manual competitor research is time-consuming and produces inconsistent results. This skill automates data collection, SWOT analysis, and gap reporting so teams can make strategic decisions faster and with better structure.
actual_capabilities: Generate structured SWOT analysis for any list of competitors
Compare product features and pricing across competing companies
Produce gap analysis reports highlighting market opportunities
Summarize competitor strengths and weaknesses from public knowledge
Create structured competitive landscape overviews for strategic planning
limitations: Relies on publicly available training data and not live web search
Cannot access proprietary, paywalled, or real-time market data
Analysis depth depends on the specificity and detail of user input
Outputs require human review before use in strategic decisions

use_cases.title: Startup Competitive Research
use_cases.description: Founders quickly map the competitive landscape before entering a new market segment.

use_cases.title: Product Manager Feature Comparison
use_cases.description: Product managers compare feature sets across competitors to identify gaps and differentiation opportunities.

use_cases.title: Marketing Strategy Briefing
use_cases.description: Marketing teams generate competitive briefs to inform positioning strategy and campaign messaging.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Competitor Overview
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these competitors: [Company A], [Company B], [Company C]. Identify their key strengths and weaknesses in the market.

prompt_templates.title: SWOT Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a detailed SWOT analysis for [Company Name] compared to its top three competitors. Focus on product features, pricing strategy, and market positioning.

prompt_templates.title: Feature Gap Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the key features of [Product 1] and [Product 2]. Create a feature comparison matrix and identify gaps where one outperforms the other. Suggest opportunities for differentiation.

prompt_templates.title: Market Landscape Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Map the top five competitors in the [Industry] market by market position, unique selling points, and target customer segments. Include data-driven recommendations for competitive differentiation and market entry strategy.

output_examples.input: Analyze the top competitors for a project management SaaS tool targeting mid-size companies
output: SWOT analysis table with Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, and Threats for each competitor
Feature comparison matrix across pricing tiers, integrations, and collaboration tools
Gap analysis highlighting underserved market segments and feature opportunities

output_examples.input: Compare the pricing and positioning strategies of three leading e-commerce platforms
output: Pricing tier comparison across all plans with value assessment per tier
Target customer segment analysis for each platform
Strategic recommendations for competitive pricing and market positioning

best_practices: Provide specific competitor names and your own product details for the most accurate and actionable analysis
Use SWOT output as a discussion starting point and validate key claims with your own market knowledge
Combine multiple analysis types such as SWOT plus gap analysis for comprehensive competitive intelligence
anti_patterns: Do not treat the output as your sole source of competitive intelligence without independent verification
Avoid analyzing too many competitors at once as this produces shallow results across all of them
Do not expect live or real-time data since the skill draws from the AI model training knowledge

faq.question: What types of companies can this skill analyze?
faq.answer: Any company or product with sufficient public information available in the AI training data, including SaaS, e-commerce, fintech, and service businesses.

faq.question: Does this skill access live data from the internet?
faq.answer: No. The skill uses the AI model knowledge base and does not make live web requests or access external APIs.

faq.question: Can I analyze competitors in any industry?
faq.answer: Yes. The analysis framework is industry-agnostic, though result quality depends on the amount of public information available for that industry.

faq.question: How many competitors should I include in one analysis?
faq.answer: Three to five competitors per analysis produces the most useful depth. Including too many reduces the quality and specificity of each assessment.

faq.question: Is this skill available in languages other than Chinese?
faq.answer: Yes. You can write prompts in any language supported by Claude, and the skill will respond in the same language you use.

faq.question: How does this differ from using a standard Claude chat for competitor research?
faq.answer: This skill provides structured analysis frameworks and templates optimized for competitive research, producing more consistent and actionable outputs than general chat.
